title: rubus × leucophaeatus
intro: Rubus × leucophaeatus is a hybrid species of the Rubus genus, commonly known as the white-berried black raspberry. This plant is primarily found in the mountainous regions of Europe and Asia, and its fruits have been used in traditional medicine for centuries due to their medicinal properties. The Rubus × leucophaeatus plant is rich in antioxidants, flavonoids, and anthocyanins, which contribute to its health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties. The herbal preparations of Rubus × leucophaeatus include infusions, tinctures, and jams, which can be used to support digestive health, boost the immune system, and provide relief from pain and inflammation.
